note: MTA's OWN per-station accessibility description for the LIRR/MNR PARTIAL stations, giving the specific reason a station is only partially step-free (almost always: each platform is ramp-accessible, but there is no accessible path BETWEEN the two platforms, so a rider must be dropped off on the correct side). Used by scripts/rail-station-ada.mts to replace the generic station-level PARTIAL sentence with MTA's own text. PROVENANCE: scraped 2026-07-22 from the `field--name-field-ada-information` field on mta.info/stations/<slug> via a real browser (mta.info's WAF 403s curl / WebFetch / Node fetch, so this is a MANUAL snapshot that rail:station-ada cannot auto-refresh — re-scrape via the in-app Browser pane with a same-origin fetch of each station page). Keyed by station code = our rail stationExternalId. A few obvious typos in MTA's source text were corrected (Farifield -> Fairfield; 'are are' -> 'are'); otherwise verbatim.

details.2MA: The platform is accessible by ramp, but there is no accessible path between the platforms. Vehicular drop-off and/or pick-up is suggested. The nearest fully accessible stations on this line are Larchmont and Harrison.
details.2PH: The platform is accessible by ramp, but there is no accessible path between the platforms. Vehicular drop-off and/or pick-up is suggested. The nearest fully accessible stations on this line are Mount Vernon East and New Rochelle.

details.DGL: This station is accessible via ramps, but there is no accessible path between the platforms. Vehicular drop-off and/or pick-up is suggested. The nearest stations on this branch with an accessible crossover are Bayside and Little Neck. The station has tactile warning strips and audiovisual passenger information systems.
